title: HTS Market Set to Reach $76B as Industry Enters Terabit Era
summary: The global High Throughput Satellites (HTS) market is projected to reach $76 billion by 2034, driven by a surge in demand to 218 Tbps. Novaspace's latest report highlights the industry's transition into the terabit era.

title: Hughes targets sovereign satcom demand with network control software
summary: Hughes is addressing sovereign satellite communications demand with AI-enabled network control software and modems, catering to militaries transitioning to hybrid networks.

title: Golden Dome and the velocity race: Why ground-based optics are the key to mission persistence
summary: The Golden Dome mission for America is a complex systems engineering challenge for missile defense, involving hypersonic glide vehicles and decoys that compress timelines. Ground-based optics are highlighted as critical to maintaining mission persistence under these conditions.

title: NASA to test nuclear electric propulsion with 2028 mission to Mars
summary: NASA plans to test nuclear electric propulsion with a mission to Mars in 2028, utilizing hardware initially designed for the lunar Gateway.

title: Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding
summary: The U.S. Space Force is considering alternative launch options due to a potential months-long grounding of the Vulcan rocket. Officials are also exploring extending the lifespan of satellites already in orbit.

title: Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal
summary: The Office of Space Commerce has introduced a proposal for a 'light touch' approach to mission authorization to regulate new commercial space applications. This is the latest effort to establish guidelines for emerging space industry activities.

title: Terran Orbital Introduces New Star Tracker Product Line at SATSHOW 2026
summary: Terran Orbital Corporation, a Lockheed Martin company, unveiled a new star tracker product line at SATSHOW 2026. This expansion strengthens their portfolio in satellite technology.

title: Telesat eyes defense role with laser comms test ahead of LEO debut
summary: Telesat is expanding into defense applications by testing laser communications ahead of its planned low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite deployment. The company also plans a 2027 demonstration for NASA while the Pentagon seeks faster space data transport solutions.

title: Sift raises $42 million in Series B round
summary: Sift, a Southern California startup, raised $42 million in a Series B investment round to expand its engineering team and develop infrastructure for AI-controlled devices. The funding will support tools that help engineers interpret hardware sensor data, particularly for applications like modern rockets and satellites.

title: Infinity Avionics Unveils Aquila: The Next-Generation Space Imaging Solution for Complex Orbital Operations
summary: Infinity Avionics launched Aquila, a next-generation space imaging solution for complex orbital operations. The product was unveiled at IAC Sydney and is now commercially available.

title: Rattling the Roman Space Telescope | Space photo of the day for March 26, 2026
summary: NASA released a new image of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope undergoing vibration testing to simulate launch conditions. The testing is part of preparations for the telescope's future mission.

title: Incredible new NASA images reveal Saturn in a new light — and it's all thanks to a telescope team-up from Webb and Hubble
summary: NASA's James Webb and Hubble space telescopes have captured unprecedented images of Saturn, revealing its atmosphere and rings in unprecedented detail. This collaborative observation marks the most detailed view of the planet to date.

title: Who is Artemis? Meet the Greek goddess who inspired NASA's return to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis program is named after the Greek goddess of the moon, drawing inspiration from Greek mythology to symbolize humanity's return to the lunar surface with missions like Artemis 2. The program aims to connect the legacy of the Apollo missions to future lunar exploration.

title: These 45 exoplanets may be the best places to search for alien life
summary: A new catalog identifies 45 exoplanets that could be prime candidates for searching for alien life. This provides a framework for future observations in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Aim for the stars for under $15 with this Estes 2441 Mini Arcas Model Rocket Kit at Walmart, now 17% cheaper in the spring sales
summary: Walmart is offering a 17% discount on the Estes 2441 Mini Arcas Model Rocket Kit, allowing enthusiasts to launch a mini-mission in solidarity with the Artemis 2 crew. The deal is part of spring sales, making space hobbyist kits more affordable.

title: Lightning on Jupiter could be up to 1 million times stronger than on Earth
summary: A new study suggests that lightning on Jupiter could be up to 1 million times stronger than on Earth, with some bolts potentially exceeding 100 times the power of terrestrial lightning.

title: Inside Artemis 2: NASA's historic astronaut moon mission explained (video series)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, a historic astronaut moon mission, is explained in a four-part video series by Space.com debuting from March 25 to March 31. The series provides an in-depth look at the mission's objectives and preparations.

title: 100 new alien worlds: Scientists find hidden haul in data from NASA exoplanet-hunting spacecraft
summary: Astronomers discovered 100 new exoplanets using data from NASA's TESS spacecraft and an AI program. This expands the known catalog of alien worlds beyond our solar system.

title: Jupiter and the moon take a sunset stroll tonight. Here's how to see it
summary: Jupiter and the Moon will appear close together in the sky during sunset on March 26. This celestial event can be observed with the naked eye.

title: Canada cancels its 1st moon rover: 'It's hopefully not a lost cause'
summary: Canada has canceled its first-ever moon rover mission, which was planned to land near the lunar south pole in 2029. The decision leaves the mission's future uncertain despite hopes it could still proceed.

title: La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna
summary: NASA announced coverage plans for the Artemis II mission, a crewed flight around the Moon. The launch is targeted for no earlier than April 1, 2024, with a two-hour launch window.

title: NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope detected the first-ever spin reversal of a tiny comet, showing how volatile activity can alter the rotation of small solar system bodies. This marks a significant observation in understanding the physical evolution of comets.

title: La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la misión lunar Artemis II
summary: NASA announces continuous coverage updates for the Artemis II lunar mission, including pre-launch, launch, and mission operations events.

title: NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews
summary: NASA Ames Research Center is offering media interviews with experts ahead of the Artemis II flight test, which will send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in over 50 years. The interviews are scheduled for March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon
summary: NASA has announced coverage for the Artemis II mission, a crewed flight around the Moon, with a launch window opening on April 1. The mission marks a significant step in NASA's Artemis program.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission
summary: NASA has announced live coverage for the upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon, with a targeted launch no earlier than 6:24 p.m. EDT on Wednesday, April. The agency will provide continuous updates and live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events.

title: Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson
summary: NASA dedicated an Artemis Moon Tree at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in March 2026, honoring the legacy of Mary W. Jackson. The loblolly pine sapling was planted months prior and symbolizes NASA's Artemis program.

title: NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager
summary: NASA appointed Jennifer Lyons as the acting program manager for the Launch Services Program (LSP) at Kennedy Space Center. She will oversee the acquisition and management of commercial launch services for science and robotic missions starting April 1.

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released its FY25 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) outlining service descriptions, chargeback rates, and service level indicators for NSSC services.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company had planned to proceed with the launch but faced an unexpected delay.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for 2028, with Administrator Jared Isaacman and agency leaders detailing the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is rapidly progressing on ground infrastructure at Starbase in preparation for the Block 3 Starship variant. This effort highlights the company's focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired pad. The mission follows repairs made to the launch site after previous operations.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. This marks a significant expansion in the company's space infrastructure ambitions.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have reached the launch pad in preparation for the Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the historic lunar mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2 in preparation for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This marks another milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of space launches is scheduled with nine launches planned, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches are highlighted in a preview by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the space industry news, but no specific details about the content or event are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is operational and responding.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. These events aim to engage the industrial community and facilitate direct exchange on follow-up activities.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use this new costing software in their financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a football-sized, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates microgravity conditions for testing robotic and spacecraft maneuvers. This initiative aims to support educational and research activities in space technology.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's changing vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs) following a proposal by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has raised concerns and confusion among legislators about the future of America's human presence in low Earth orbit.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions, aiming to streamline regulatory authority amid ongoing debates over mission authorization frameworks.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space secured $40 million in funding to advance in-space propulsion technology using storable bipropellant, aiming to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. The company aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital activity rises.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has revised NASA's priorities to focus on a commercially driven lunar economy and surface infrastructure. This marks a significant shift in the agency's strategic direction.

title: One-On-One With SDA’s GP Sandhoo
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) launched its first operational tranche of satellites six months ago, but troops are not yet benefiting due to minor issues forcing a 'strategic pause'. SDA Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo discussed these challenges in an interview with Payload.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio Hosted Payload Platform
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio hosted payload platform, a new model where payloads can be attached to and detached from satellites in orbit. This approach aims to provide flexibility for satellite operations.

title: Ramon.Space, Ingrasys Aim To Fly Prototype Orbital Data Center In 2027
summary: Ramon.Space and Ingrasys plan to launch a prototype orbital data center in 2027, joining the growing competition in space-based data infrastructure.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission relies on advanced digital infrastructure unseen by the public, marking a significant evolution from the Apollo era. NASA's digital systems are crucial for the mission's success, as highlighted by Booz Allen's Karen Fields.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles satellite connectivity services from Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and SpaceX's Starlink, allowing customers to use both networks without choosing one over the other.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking space-collected data to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades, with demand driving further development in the sector.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are expected to drive the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. This trend highlights the pivotal role of public sector involvement in shaping the industry's future.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems at SATShow Week, featuring live multi-orbit demonstrations.

title: Space Commerce Official Proposes Streamlined Mission Authorization Process for Novel Space Missions
summary: U.S. Office of Space Commerce Director Taylor Jordan proposed a streamlined mission authorization process for novel space activities during SATShow Week. The initiative aims to simplify regulations for emerging space missions.

title: Inside the Data Fusion and Integration Challenges to Building the Golden Dome
summary: The article discusses the data fusion and integration challenges in developing the Golden Dome missile defense system, which involves tracking missile launches and relaying data to interceptors. The Golden Dome aims to provide 3D tracking for ballistic and hypersonic threats.

title: Geopolitical Shakeups and Capital Wars Push Top Operators to Iterate Faster
summary: Geopolitical conflicts and rising concerns are highlighting the critical role of satellite communications, prompting top operators to accelerate innovation. Commercial satellite leaders emphasize the need for faster iteration in response to these global developments.

title: Launchers Cite Busy Manifests and Scarcity as Commercial Demand Grows
summary: Launch providers report extremely busy launch manifests for the next two years due to growing commercial demand. SpaceX is highlighted as a key player in this competitive heavy-lift launch market.

title: Lynk and SES CEOs Talk D2D Demand, As D2D Merger Awaits Completion
summary: The CEOs of Lynk and SES discussed the booming demand for direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity despite market competition and geopolitical challenges. The merger of the two companies in the D2D sector is still pending completion.

title: SDA Takes ‘Strategic Pause’ on Launches to Work Through Fixes
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused launches to address and implement necessary fixes. This strategic pause aims to refine processes and ensure smoother future operations.
